findings.detail: Imports from sibling shadow data modules: shadow_data_types (types), shadow_schema (DDL), shadow_trade_repo, shadow_snapshot_repo, shadow_analysis_repo, shadow_integrity_repo. These are all data-layer modules — no circular dependencies. No imports from pipeline/. No imports from gateway/.

ShadowStateDB is the single persistence layer for all shadow data. Methods delegate to specialized repos (trade, snapshot, analysis, integrity) but present a unified API. Connection management: each method opens/closes its own connection — no connection pooling, no stale connections.

get_ranking_eligible_shadows() is the canonical filter for ranking/collusion/challenger pipelines. Excludes: beta (experimental, unranked), retired (frozen benchmarks), eliminated (removed), challenger (opaque trial). This filter ensures Phase 2 shadow lifecycle boundaries are enforced at the data layer.

update_methodology_prompt() logs all changes to methodology_changes table with old/new prompt (truncated to 500 chars), reason, and timestamp. get_methodology_history() and get_original_methodology() support audit trail. This is P1-1 compliance.

ShadowStateDB class is the single interface. Complete CRUD for shadows, trades, snapshots, rankings, integrity events, emergency quotas, collusion flags. Beta analyses isolated in separate table/methods.
